text: Good team management relies on four core skills: communication, delegation, feedback, and motivation. Clear communication ensures everyone knows their tasks and goals, preventing misunderstandings. Delegation involves assigning tasks based on team members' skills, not just distributing work evenly. Regular feedback helps individuals improve and feel valued, but it must be constructive. Motivation keeps the team engaged—simple recognition of effort often works better than large rewards. Conflict resolution is equally important; when disagreements arise, address them quickly by listening to each side and finding a fair solution. A manager who balances these elements creates a productive, supportive environment where the team can thrive.
